concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Karachi leads Pakistan's urban system with 24.7% of tracked urban population across 278 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Rawalpindi & Islamabad
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Rawalpindi and Islamabad (11 km apart, combined population 3,113,056). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 40 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Pakistan sits between distributed and dominant: Karachi leads with 24.7% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Rawalpindi → Islamabad (40 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
